programmes.show_description: In this compilation episode focussing on older patients, paramedics treat a 103-year-old man who's collapsed and a driver who's had two crashes in a week but has no memory of what happened.
Some patients at King's have obvious symptoms while others initially baffle the medical team. Consultant Graham thinks Icylyn's bloated abdomen could be due to something serious.
Bobby, 48, is brought to King's by his partner after having seizures at home. Ronald, 70, is having problems breathing. Rienelle, who's seven, has fallen and landed on her back.
On day three in Barcelona, Angela takes her fellow guests to a vineyard to sample the local vino. The wine flows all day, but by dinner the conversation's a little worse for wear. (Ep13/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
The B&Bers meet for one last time to find out how much they've been paid and to thrash out their differences. There's trouble as Mrs Tee and Alistair and Emma clash over their scores.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: In Surrey, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien meet petrolhead Graham, who collects Kawasaki motorbikes.
Gary and Jennifer are starting a new chapter in their lives by buying a holiday home near the Spanish town of Vera, on the Costa de Almería. Ben Hillman shows them what their £55k can buy.
Aberdeen. Glaswegian offshore logistics supervisor Michelle hopes to impress her guests with her 'Weegie' wit and the mother of all balloon games. (Ep1/5)
Aberdeen. Confident economics undergraduate David hosts with a 1920s Gatsby theme. His dress code goes down a storm, but will his student grub impress? (Ep2/5)
Aberdeen. Oil and gas buying specialist Sylvian hopes to wow her guests with her ambitious East-meets-West menu. But her toffee pudding turns out to be sticky in more ways than one. (Ep3/5)
Aberdeen. Comedy promoter Naz has his work cut out to pull off the winning party: he's managed to offend nearly all of his guests during the week, and he's not the greatest cook... (Ep4/5)
Aberdeen. Journalist Turid serves up a menu of home-grown produce, on a night featuring shocking revelations, farmyard antics and plenty of laughter. (Ep5/5)
The first visit of the week is to Abbey Court Guest House in Carlisle. Host Kelly aims to impress with plush rooms and abseiling. But will breakfast complaints send her chances off a cliff?
Glam couple Rob and Heloisa host at The Lime Tree Inn in Yorkshire, where chef Rob pulls out all the stops at breakfast. It's a shame the cleaners didn't do the same in Kelly's bathroom.
Visit three is to Holly Grove Farm in Staffordshire. Hosts Dave and Karon think that their rural glampsite is a tucked-away idyll, and their guests concur - apart from one...
Can host Pav handle his guests' grumbles about cleanliness and instant coffee when he hosts the week's final visit, at Beldon House B&B in Lincolnshire?
One B&Ber comes under fire on payment day for some suspicious scoring, while a confusing breakfast rating is high on the agenda for another. But most importantly, who's won the week?
Ford Escort RS2000: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend visit Dorset to fix up a Mk2 RS2000. It belongs to carpenter Mike, who gave up work to support his son who had a terrible accident.
Saab 900: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Loughborough to rescue Pravesh's 1992 Saab 900 Turbo that's in desperate need of repair.
Kirstie Allsopp helps Jay and Charlene upsize in Essex, and Phil Spencer's with Jemma and Graham, who returned from LA three years ago and now want to settle in Southend-on-Sea.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Croydon, Kirstie and Phil may have met their match: a swimming pool in the lounge. Can Kirstie help Christine love her bungalow or will Phil convince Tony to move?
Brand new series - Ghosts: A body is found at the home of Liguori's Uncle Edoardo, but the elderly eccentric blames the spirits that have been haunting him. In Italian/subs.
Kevin, who's 78, is rushed to A&E after going into cardiac arrest while exercising. Monica, who's 83 and has osteoporosis, has been rushed to King's after falling at home.
A look at the work of the King's stroke team, who treat over 1300 patients a year. And the UK's over-40s BMX champion visits A&E after falling 20 feet when a stunt went wrong.
The East Anglian Air Ambulance crew battle to save driver Gerry's partially amputated hand. Motorcyclist Jamie has sustained multiple injuries following a collision with a van.
Kevin, who's 78, is rushed to A&E after going into cardiac arrest while exercising. Monica, who's 83 and has osteoporosis, has been rushed to King's after falling at home.
On day four in Barcelona, Lubomba opts for some jet skiing fun, but not everyone is keen to take the plunge. And some cuisine confusion causes tension at dinner. (Ep14/20)
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Discover the very latest must-haves for your home and garden with Must Have Ideas.
Dazzling décor, a super night's sleep and an eggcellent breakfast impress most - but not all - of the guests at Antoinette and Carmen's Seacrest Hotel in Portsmouth.
Best mates Glenn and Leon host at The White Horse Hotel in Storrington, West Sussex. There are hair-raising concerns and damp discoveries, which are too close for comfort for some guests.
Husband and wife Mike and Del offer a real taste of Wales at Deri-Down Guesthouse in Abergavenny. But there are bed blunders and the pre-order breakfast gets a grilling.
Margaret and her son Fraser host at Royle Farm B&B in Burton-on-Trent. Some guests fixate on the fantastical furniture, while others feel askew about the view.
It's the final day and the B&Bers meet for one last time to settle some scores. After bedtime blues and follicle findings are aired, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.
Geoff's looking for a holiday home on beautiful Barbados. Craig Rowe shows Geoff five impressive properties for his £160k, but are any of them perfect?
Candy and Mark are looking for the perfect rural retreat to escape to in Almería in southern Spain. Ben Hillman lines up five fab options for £75k, but will any of them result in an offer?
Jules and Gaynor want to move to Poitou-Charentes in France - with their pet alpacas - to run their own gîte business. Will any of Sara Damergi's options have them al-packing their bags?
Doc and Lucia want to split their time between the beautiful, whitewashed town of Alora in southern Spain, and backpacking around the world. Laura Hamilton shows them what £110k can buy.
Cardiff. DJ Trishna kicks off the competition with a hip-hop theme, on a night featuring rapping, flirting and booty shaking! (Ep1/5)
Cardiff. Fastidious host Nick plans to bring some refinement and fine dining to the week. But is his massive tasting menu overambitious? (Ep2/5)
Cardiff. Retail manager Yuno serves up some traditional dancing alongside his Nepalese food. But are his spicy dishes too fiery for his guests' delicate palates? (Ep3/5)
Cardiff. Self-appointed 'hostess with the mostest' Michelle takes her guests on a culinary trip around the world - with lamb on the menu for the third time this week. (Ep4/5)
Cardiff. Final host Suzanne plans an evening of classic French cuisine, but some of her guests feel uneasy when she reveals the saucy inspiration behind her mushroom starter... (Ep5/5)
The competition begins at The Oaks Luxury Lodges in County Londonderry. Hosts Louise and Lynsey hope that Derry's heritage will win the B&Bers over. But breakfast proves divisive.
Bridie hosts the second visit of the week at Pine Tree Hollow Glamping in Leitrim. She's keen to show her guests that back to basics doesn't mean boring - but not everyone's convinced.
The B&Bers are off glamping for a second time, at Isabel and Aisling's Ness Wood Glamping in County Londonderry. But the pristine pods are too petite for some.
Husband-and-wife team Bridie and James host the week's final visit at Dulrush Fishing Lodge in County Fermanagh, where the hosts are left reeling by some confusing feedback.
It's payment day, and the B&Bers meet up one last time to settle some scores. Expect breakfast bombshells and tit-for-tat tussles before the payments are revealed and we find out who's won.
Brian, who's 74, is taken to King's by ambulance after falling down some stairs. His partner Margaret discusses their life together and the challenges of his severe dementia.
This episode meets older patients at King's hospital who've had to show resilience in their lives. And junior doctor Jim tries to get some Lego out of toddler Zara's nose.
The heli-medics struggle to free Malcolm, who's trapped in his mangled car. When there's a motorbike accident in the Peaks, medics rush to help Jamie, who's fractured his arm.
